title: Wrong datatype being persisted when inserting both booleans and integer types
description:
When inserting triples using SPARQL, I have observed some strange behaviour when triples with booleans and integer datatypes other than xsd:integer are involved. After some experimentation it looks to me like triples can be persisted with the wrong datatype if the following occurs in an INSERT query:

there is a triple with a boolean value
there is a triple with an integer value using a datatype different from xsd:integer
the two triples' values "match" (false and 0, or true and 1)

The order of the triples in the query affect which datatype is persisted.
I have included the results of some experiments. The insert query used was this:
PREFIX :<http://www.dummy.com/ontology#>
PREFIX xsd:<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
INSERT {
  GRAPH  <http://www.dummy.com> {
    <http://www.dummy.com/1> :prop1 {prop1} .
    <http://www.dummy.com/1> :prop2 {prop2} .
  }
}

The results were then read using:
SELECT ?s ?p ?o
FROM <http://www.dummy.com>
WHERE {
  ?s ?p ?o
}

The graph was emptied between experiments.
Following is a table containing the values tested for {prop1} and {prop2}, the results and some comments.



prop1
prop2
Result
Comment




"true"^^xsd:boolean
"1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger
http://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop1 "true"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#booleanhttp://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop2 "true"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#boolean
having the boolean triple first in the query results in two booleans


"1"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger
"true"^^xsd:boolean
http://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop1 "1"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#nonNegativeIntegerhttp://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop2 "1"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#nonNegativeInteger
having the xsd:nonNegativeInteger triple first in the query results in two xsd:nonNegativeIntegers


"true"^^xsd:boolean
"0"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger
http://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop1 "true"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#booleanhttp://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop2 "0"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#nonNegativeInteger
when the boolean value doesn't "match" the integer value (0 and true), everything is OK


"false"^^xsd:boolean
"0"^^xsd:integer
http://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop1 "false"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#booleanhttp://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop2 0
when using xsd:integer, everything is OK


"false"^^xsd:boolean
"0"^^xsd:int
http://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop1 "false"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#booleanhttp://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop2 "false"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#boolean
using xsd:int yields the same results as using xsd:nonNegativeInteger


"false"^^xsd:boolean
"0"^^xsd:intz (made up datatype)
http://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop1 "false"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#booleanhttp://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop2 "0"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#intz
using the made up datatype xsd:intz works fine, suggesting Virtuoso is handling xsd:int and xsd:nonNegativeInteger (and maybe others) in some special way


"0"^^xsd:nonNegativeInteger
"0"^^xsd:integer
http://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop1 "0"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#nonNegativeIntegerhttp://www.dummy.com/1 http://www.dummy.com/ontology#prop2 0
when no booleans are involved, everything is OK



All queries were run on version 07.20.3229-pthreads for Linux, using the web UI available at the SPARQL endpoint.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY, prop1 BOOLEAN, prop2 INT UNSIGNED);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, prop1, prop2) VALUES ('http://www.dummy.com/1', TRUE, 1);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, prop1, prop2) VALUES ('http://www.dummy.com/2', TRUE, 1);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, prop1, prop2) VALUES ('http://www.dummy.com/3', FALSE, 0);
SELECT id, prop1, prop2, CASE WHEN prop1 IS TRUE THEN 'boolean' ELSE 'not boolean' END AS datatype1, CASE WHEN prop2 IS NOT NULL THEN 'integer' ELSE 'null' END AS datatype2 FROM test_table;
key_bug_features: Order of triples affects persisted datatype in INSERT query
